hypotheses.name: Zero-norm validity check: TwoNN and SVD on nonzero-only genes
hypotheses.method: Computed norms at all 12 layers. Found 2902 genes have zero norm at ALL layers (same constant set). TwoNN on 2039 nonzero-only genes: L0→L11. SVD eff_rank on nonzero-only at L0 and L11.
hypotheses.primary_metric: TwoNN ID and SVD eff_rank on nonzero-only genes
hypotheses.result_value: TwoNN: L0=34.7 → L11=20.0 (ratio=0.58, validated). SVD eff_rank: L0=236.3 → L11=48.4 (4.9x collapse, corrects prior 14x claim). Zero genes are structurally absent at all layers.
hypotheses.next_action: TwoNN compression claim is validated and corrected. SVD eff_rank should be updated in paper. Characterize what the 2039 nonzero vs 2902 zero genes represent biologically (expression level, coverage).

hypotheses.name: SV1 identity: what does the dominant direction encode?
hypotheses.method: SVD of centered nonzero-gene embeddings at L11. Check SV1/SV2 explained variance fractions. Attempt lineage marker projection onto SV1/SV2 axes. Gene names file missing so biological annotation blocked.
hypotheses.primary_metric: SV1 and SV2 variance fractions in nonzero-only genes
hypotheses.result_value: SV1=18.6% variance, SV2=11.7% variance (nonzero-only). Prior claim of 93.4% for SV1 was dominated by zero vectors. Relatively distributed spectrum, not dominated by single axis.
hypotheses.next_action: Find gene names file to do biological annotation. Prior SV1-dominance finding from iter_0046 was an artifact of zero vectors. With nonzero-only genes, spectrum is much more distributed (SV1=18.6%).
hypotheses.retirement_reason: Blocked by missing gene names file; prior finding invalidated for nonzero-only analysis

hypotheses.name: ID compression breakpoint detection across layers
hypotheses.method: TwoNN at each of 12 layers on 2039 nonzero-only genes. Piecewise linear fit to find breakpoint layer. Finite differences for per-layer drops.
hypotheses.primary_metric: Global linear R2 and breakpoint layer
hypotheses.result_value: ID decreases nearly linearly (R2=0.986, slope=-1.30/layer). Best piecewise breakpoint at L5 (slopes: -1.61 before, -1.22 after). Largest single drops at L3 (delta=-2.43) and L9 (delta=-1.94). No sharp phase transition.
hypotheses.next_action: Compression is gradual and linear, not phasic. No strong evidence for discrete geometry change points. Record as context for paper: smooth monotone compression, not step-function.
